title: Identifying Tokenizer Inconsistencies
concept: If your training data was tokenized with a different vocabulary table than your model expects, the input IDs will map to garbage embeddings, causing training collapse.

exercises.prompt: What is the main job of a tokenizer in general the main job of a tokenizer in-game main job of a tokenizer in AI?

options.text: To convert words into numbers (tokens/IDs) for the model
To translate English text directly into Spanish
To increase the speed of the internet connection

exercises.explain: A tokenizer translates human words into numbers (IDs) that the computer can process.

cards.title: The Wrong Key
cards.body: Have you ever tried to unlock a door with a key that fits, but doesn't turn? In AI, using the wrong tokenizer is just like using the wrong key. The numbers go in, but the meaning is completely locked away, leading to a total training disaster!

cards.title: What is Tokenizer Inconsistency?
cards.body: A tokenizer inconsistency happens when your input text is converted into numbers using one vocabulary table, but your model tries to read those numbers using a different vocabulary table. Because the numbers point to the wrong words, the model receives chaotic, incorrect signals.

cards.title: The Wrong Dictionary
cards.body: Imagine buying a translation dictionary for French-to-English, but using it to translate a book written in Spanish! The page numbers and word locations won't match up at all. You will end up reading random, meaningless gibberish. The same thing happens when a model uses the wrong vocabulary table to read tokenized text.

cards.title: A Numeric Mix-up
cards.body: If Tokenizer A assigns the number 42 to the word 'apple', but Tokenizer B thinks 42 means 'banana', sending a 42 to a model trained on Tokenizer B will cause it to read 'banana' instead of 'apple'. Multiply this by millions of words, and your training data becomes complete nonsense!

cards.title: Myth: Models Can 'Figure It Out'
cards.body: Many think that if two tokenizers both output numbers, the model can easily adapt and learn the differences. This is false! If the vocabularies don't match, the input IDs map to completely wrong word embeddings, causing training to collapse immediately.

cards.title: Comparing Vocabularies
cards.body: Look at how a simple word gets translated into IDs. If the IDs don't match the model's vocabulary, the model reads the wrong words!

right.text: Text: 'Hello' -> Tokenizer B: ID 99 -> Model A reads: 'Garbage'

left.text: Text: 'Hello' -> Tokenizer A: ID 12 -> Model A reads: 'Hello'

exercises.prompt: What happens if your training data is tokenized with a different vocabulary than your model expects?

options.text: The training will just run slightly slower.
The input IDs map to garbage embeddings, causing training collapse.
The model automatically fixes the vocabulary mismatch.
The model will only output punctuation marks.

exercises.explain: If the IDs point to the wrong locations in the embedding table, the model gets garbage data, causing the training process to collapse.

exercises.prompt: True or False: To train a model correctly, the tokenizer used to prepare the dataset must match the model's expected vocabulary.
exercises.explain: Every tokenizer has its own unique mapping of words to numbers. Using a different one will feed incorrect meanings into the model.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of this training error.
exercises.sentence: Using a different vocabulary table for preprocessing and training causes a tokenizer ________.
exercises.explain: A tokenizer inconsistency happens when the vocabulary used to convert text to IDs does not match what the model expects, leading to garbage inputs.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following sounds like an AI explaining a tokenizer inconsistency?

options.text: System Log: 'WARN: Tokenizer mismatch detected. Expected vocab size 50257, got 32000.'
AI Chatbot: 'Oh no! My training is collapsing because I am interpreting your tokens using the wrong vocabulary dictionary. I am seeing gibberish!'

exercises.explain: AI Chatbots are conversational and generated by LLMs. System Logs are simple technical logs, and Config code contains setup parameters. Option C sounds exactly like an AI explaining its internal state.

exercises.prompt: Your team is preparing to train a model, but you realize the dataset was pre-tokenized using 'Tokenizer-A' while your model is initialized with 'Tokenizer-B'. How do you prevent a total training collapse?

options.text: Train a brand new tokenizer from scratch during training
Verify and align the tokenizer vocabulary tables before training
Increase the learning rate to bypass the garbage embeddings
Add more layers to the transformer model structure

exercises.explain: Always double-check that your dataset's vocabulary exactly matches your model's vocabulary! If they mismatch, the IDs point to incorrect embeddings, causing the model to learn complete garbage and fail.
